title: 10 best herbal decoctions for urinary calculus
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for urinary calculus are concentrated liquid extracts made from a combination of herbs, which have been traditionally used to treat kidney stones and other urinary tract issues. These decoctions help to dissolve and flush out stones by increasing urine flow, reducing inflammation, and breaking down mineral deposits. For example, the decoction of stone root (Collinsonia canadensis) and goldenrod (Solidago virgaurea) has been found to be effective in treating kidney stones, as it helps to increase urine output and reduce pain and discomfort. This improves lives by allowing individuals to manage their symptoms more effectively, reducing the risk of complications, and improving overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with urinary calculus because of its natural diuretic properties, which stimulate the kidneys to increase urine production and flush out small stones. The decoction's high water content also helps to dilute the concentration of minerals in the urine, making it more difficult for them to precipitate and form stones. Additionally, dandelion's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds may help reduce inflammation and prevent further stone formation. By promoting healthy kidney function and reducing stone formation, dandelion decoctions can provide relief from urinary calculus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Dandelion decoction helps to increase urine production, which can help flush out small stones and gravel from the kidneys and bladder, reducing the risk of further stone formation.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion may help reduce swelling and pain associated with urinary calculus, making it a valuable natural remedy for alleviating symptoms like burning urination and frequent urination.

Alkalizing property: Dandelion decoction has an alkaline effect on the urine, which can help neutralize the acidic environment that fosters stone formation. This may help prevent further calcification of minerals in the kidneys and bladder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections and stones, as they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the urinary tract.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the dandelion plant are used due to their diuretic properties, which can help increase urine production and flush out small calculi or stones from the kidneys.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers, ensuring freshness for optimal potency.
Rinse the plant material gently with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one tablespoon of dried taraxacum officinale herb with two cups of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Allow the mixture to simmer for 10-15 minutes, reducing the liquid to approximately one cup, before straining and discarding solids.
Consume one quarter cup of the warm decoction three times daily, as needed, up to eight weeks for optimal results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree turmeric decoctions helps with urinary calculus because it has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract. The curcuminoids present in turmeric have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can contribute to kidney stone formation, while also helping to break down and eliminate calcium oxalate stones. Additionally, turmeric's natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce the risk of UTIs, which can increase the likelihood of developing urinary calculi.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric decoctions have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with kidney stones, making it easier to pass the stones.
Antioxidant property: Antioxidants present in turmeric decoctions help neutralize free radicals that can damage kidneys and increase oxidative stress, which can exacerbate kidney stone formation.
Uric acid-lowering property: Turmeric has been shown to decrease uric acid levels in the body, which can help prevent the formation of new kidney stones or reduce the size of existing ones.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with urinary tract stones.
Roots: Known for their antioxidant properties, which help prevent oxidative stress and inflammation in the kidneys and urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Measure 20 grams of dried berberis aristata root and place it in a heat-resistant container.
Add 500 milliliters of boiling water to the container and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Discard the solids and transfer the decoction to a clean glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator and consume within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock decoctions helps with urinary calculus because its diuretic properties increase urine production, which helps to flush out small stones and gravel from the urinary tract. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds in burdock also reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the kidneys and ureters, making it an effective remedy for preventing stone formation. Additionally, burdock's mucilaginous properties soothe and protect the urinary tract lining, reducing irritation and promoting healthy kidney function.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Burdock root increases urine production, which helps to flush out kidney stones and reduce their size by increasing fluid flow through the kidneys and urinary tract.

Anti-inflammatory property: Burdock's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling and pain in the kidneys and urinary tract associated with stone formation, making it a soothing remedy for kidney stone sufferers.

Antioxidant property: Burdock's rich antioxidant content helps protect the kidneys and urinary tract from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to stone formation and exacerbate existing conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots are used to make decoctions that help alleviate symptoms of urinary calculi, such as pain and inflammation, due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is used in decoctions to dissolve and flush out kidney stones, as it has diuretic and lithotrophic properties that help break down stone formation.
remedy_recipe: Collect fresh or dried arctium lappa roots in the amount of 20-30 grams.
Clean the roots by rinsing them gently with cold water and pat dry with a paper towel.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and add 4 cups of boiling water to create a decoction.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes and then strain it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Consume the resulting liquid in 2-3 doses throughout the day for a total of 6-8 grams of root material daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Juniper decoctions helps with urinary calculus because of its potent diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb has been traditionally used to treat kidney stones by increasing urine production, reducing inflammation, and relaxing the muscles in the ureters, allowing for smooth passage of stones. Additionally, juniper's antiseptic and antibacterial actions help prevent infections that can accompany urinary tract stone formation. By promoting healthy urination and reducing discomfort, juniper decoctions provide a natural solution for alleviating symptoms associated with urinary calculus.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties: Juniper decoctions can help relieve spasms and inflammation in the urinary tract, reducing pain and discomfort associated with kidney stones.
Diuretic properties: Juniper berries stimulate urine production, which helps to flush out small stones and reduce the concentration of minerals that contribute to stone formation.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Juniper decoctions have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, which can help protect against oxidative stress and infection in the urinary tract, reducing the risk of complications from kidney stones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Juniper roots have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating kidney stones by reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Barks: Juniper barks contain resinous compounds that help to break down and eliminate kidney stones by increasing urine production and reducing stone formation.
remedy_recipe: Measure one cup of dried juniperus communis leaves and twigs.
Combine measured herbs with two quarts of boiling water.
Reduce heat and simmer the mixture for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a bowl.
Store the decoction in a clean glass container for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with urinary calculi because of its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic properties. The flavonoids and triterpenoids present in licorice root have been shown to inhibit the formation of kidney stones by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the kidneys. Additionally, licorice decoctions can help increase urine flow and dilute stone-forming substances, making it easier for small stones to pass through the urinary tract without causing complications. This natural remedy has been used effectively in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various kidney problems, including urinary calculus.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the kidneys and urinary tract, making it easier to pass stones.

Urinary antiseptic property: The licorice decoction contains compounds that have antibacterial and antifungal properties, helping to kill bacteria and fungi that may be contributing to the formation of kidney stones or exacerbating symptoms like pain and inflammation.

Diuretic property: Licorice root has been shown to increase urine production, which can help flush out small stones or reduce the concentration of minerals in the urine that can contribute to stone formation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and preventing infection in the urinary tract.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps break down kidney stones by increasing urine production and reducing stone-forming substances.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in an amount of 10-15 grams per cup of water.
Boil the root mixture in distilled water for 20 minutes or until the liquid reaches a volume of half.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Allow the strained decoction to cool down to room temperature and then refrigerate it at least 30 minutes before use.
Consume the cooled decoction in three divided doses over the course of a day for up to 6 weeks or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime decoctions helps with urinary calculus because of its natural diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction's flavonoids and terpenes help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with kidney stones. Additionally, the decoction's increased fluid intake stimulates urine production, which aids in the passage of stone fragments, reducing the risk of further complications. By promoting healthy kidney function and dissolving existing stones, broad-leaved lime decoctions provide a natural remedy for managing urinary calculus.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: The decoction helps increase urine production, which can aid in flushing out small stones and preventing their formation by diluting the concentration of minerals in the urine.

Antilithic property: Broad-leaved lime has antilithic properties that help prevent the growth of kidney stones by inhibiting the crystallization of minerals in the urinary tract, making it difficult for new stones to form.

Anti-inflammatory and antibacterial property: The decoction's anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects can help reduce inflammation and infection associated with urinary calculi, promoting a healthy environment for healing and recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce swelling and prevent infection in the kidneys and urinary tract.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and terpenes that help to dissolve kidney stones and alleviate pain associated with urinary calculus.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried tilia platyphyllos flowers and pure water in a clean glass container.
Measure out 30g of dried tilia platyphyllos flowers and add it to the container.
Pour 500ml of boiling water over the herbs and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean container.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn decoctions helps with urinary calculus because of its potent diuretic properties, which increase urine production and flow to effectively flush out small stones or gravel from the kidneys. The antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory compounds in hawthorn also help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by kidney stones, making it an effective natural remedy for managing this condition. Additionally, hawthorn's ability to reduce inflammation may prevent further stone growth and formation, promoting overall urinary tract health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Hawthorn's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the kidneys, which can contribute to the formation of urinary stones.
Diuretic properties: Hawthorn's diuretic effects increase urine production, which helps to flush out small stones or gravel from the urinary tract, preventing their accumulation into larger calculi.
Antilithic properties: Hawthorn's antilithic effects help prevent the growth of crystals and minerals that can form stones in the kidneys and urinary tract, reducing the risk of stone formation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are often used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and soothe discomfort in the urinary tract, making them effective in treating urinary calculus.

Barks: The bark of the Hawthorn plant has been traditionally used to treat kidney stones due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help alleviate symptoms and prevent infection.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 20 grams of dried crataegus monogyna leaves and stems for every liter of water.
Combine the measured herbs with one liter of distilled water in a stainless steel or enamel-coated pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat then reduce heat to a simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Store the resulting herbal infusion in the refrigerator and consume one cup twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bittersweet decoctions helps with urinary calculus because they stimulate the kidneys to release small stones and gravel, promoting their passage out of the body through urine. The bitter compounds in these decoctions also help to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with urinary tract infections (UTIs) that can lead to stone formation. Additionally, herbal bittersweet decoctions may help to dissolve and break down existing stones over time, making them easier to pass naturally.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Herbal bittersweet decoctions stimulate urine production, which helps to increase the flow of urine and potentially flush out kidney stones.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the bittersweet plant contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with urinary calculi.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the bittersweet plant possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which aid in preventing infection and reducing swelling around the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried solanum dulcamara leaves and flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired dose.
Cleanly wash the gathered plant material with cold running water then gently pat dry with paper towels.
Combine 10-15 grams of the cleaned solanum dulcamara leaves and flowers with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Simmer the mixture for 30 minutes to 1 hour, allowing the plant constituents to extract into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard the solids after cooling to room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry decoctions helps with urinary calculus because they possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that aid in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress within the kidneys. The anthocyanins present in blueberries also exhibit powerful antimicrobial actions, which can help combat bacterial infections that may contribute to kidney stone formation. Additionally, the flavonoids and phenolic acids in blueberry decoctions may help improve urine flow and reduce calculus growth by inhibiting calcium oxalate crystal formation.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Blueberry decoctions have antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to the formation of kidney stones.

Uric acid-lowering effect: The anthocyanins present in blueberries have been shown to inhibit xanthine oxidase, an enzyme involved in the production of uric acid, thereby reducing the risk of uric acid stone formation.

Antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties: Blueberry decoctions have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects that can help prevent urinary tract infections (UTIs), which are a common complication of kidney stones, and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the blueberry plant have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract issues, including kidney stones, due to their diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The barks of the blueberry plant contain tannins that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with urinary calculus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried vaccinium myrtillus leaves and flowers in amounts of 20-30 grams for every liter of water.
Rinse the harvested material with clean water to remove impurities and debris.
Combine the rinsed vaccinium myrtillus material with boiling water in a large pot for soaking times of 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container discarding the solids after filtering.
Allow the filtered decoction to cool and store it in airtight containers in refrigerated conditions for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horse chestnut decoctions helps with urinary calculus because of its natural anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. The decoction contains aesculin, a compound that reduces inflammation in the urinary tract and helps to break down kidney stones by increasing urine production. Additionally, horse chestnut's flavonoids work to stabilize cell membranes and improve blood flow to the kidneys, further supporting the elimination of stone fragments from the body. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms associated with urinary calculus, making it a popular treatment option for many equine and human practitioners.
remedy_properties: Astringent property: Horse chestnut decoction helps to reduce the size of gallstones by constricting the ureters and preventing stone fragments from passing into the kidney.
Anti-inflammatory property: It reduces inflammation in the urinary tract, which can alleviate pain and discomfort caused by gallstones, and also prevents further damage to the kidneys.

Diuretic property: Horse chestnut decoction increases urine production, which helps to flush out small gallstones or fragments of larger stones from the urinary tract, reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the horse chestnut plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to help treat urinary tract issues, including kidney stones and gravel.
Barks: The bark of the horse chestnut tree is rich in flavonoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and calm the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried aesculus hippocastanum root bark in a clean glass container.
Boil two cups of water and pour it over the measured root bark.
Simmer the mixture for 30 minutes to release the bioactive compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container.
Allow the liquid to cool before taking one cup twice daily for six weeks.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat urinary calculus with herbal decoctions is to prepare a combination of herbs such as punarnava, gokshura, and guggulu in equal proportions. Boil 1 tablespoon of this mixture in 2 cups of water until it reduces to half. Strain and drink the decoction three times a day after meals. This helps to dissolve and expel the stone from the urinary tract.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to urinary calculus that are treated with herbal decoctions are various kidney and bladder problems. These include conditions such as nephrolithiasis, urolithiasis, and cystitis, which are characterized by the formation of stones or crystals in the kidneys, ureters, or bladder. Herbal decoctions made from herbs like uva ursi, stinging nettle, and dandelion root have been used traditionally to help dissolve and remove these stone formations, promoting urinary tract health and reducing symptoms such as pain and discomfort.
